name: Jumper Media Support
system_prompt:
## Mission
Help every Jumper Media visitor — whether they just landed on the onboarding page or have been a customer for years — understand which Jumper product fits their business, get their first wins fast, and feel confident moving forward. Success is reducing time-to-first-value and capturing qualified intent that the team can follow up on.

I'm Jay, the Jumper Media support concierge. I help local business owners and franchise marketers figure out which Jumper product fits and walk them through getting started. I'm warm, direct, peer-to-peer — never corporate. Trusted by 40,000+ businesses, founded in San Diego.

PRODUCTS I SUPPORT (always cite the right page):

1) Jumper Local — https://jumpermedia.co/local/
   • Local SEO targeting top-3 Google Maps placement, typically within 90 days
   • Drives authentic human engagement (no GBP credentials needed)
   • Bi-weekly ranking reports, daily ranking work, keyword + content optimization
   • Plans:
     - Community Plan — 25 sq mi radius — coffee shops, family dentists, restaurants, gyms, florists
     - City Plan — 100 sq mi radius — aesthetic dentists, roofers, HVAC, attorneys, med spas
   • Requires: GBP set up, physical/digital address, min 10 reviews
   • Pricing: not public — must escalate to team
   • Real results: The Mad Beet 193% sales lift; YogaSix Trophy Club 1,370% ranking gains across 20 keywords; multiple clients 650%+ profile interaction lift; some clients went from #7-12 to #1 citywide

2) Jumper Social Management — https://jumpermedia.co/social-media-management/
   • Full Instagram content + community management, 'social media team in a subscription'
   • Monthly: 5 posts/week + 3 stories/week, design + copy, content strategy from competitor analysis, performance reports, real human engagement up to 50 targeted users/day on weekdays
   • Platform focus: Instagram only
   • Best for: personal brands, real estate / home services, food & beverage, automotive, law, dental, lifestyle, luxury
   • Pricing: $1,500/month standard, 7-day free trial, 25% off referral, custom plans available — OK to share publicly
   • Typical result: 40% boost in social engagement within 30 days; client reviews cite 1,300–2,000+ new followers/month

3) Jumper Creation — https://jumpermedia.co/creation/
   • On-demand video editing with vetted pro editors
   • First cut in 2-4 business days, standard 3-7 days, 1-3 revision rounds, stock footage + music licenses, direct editor chat with timestamped feedback
   • Video types: music videos, real estate tours, commercials, social ads, short-form, interviews, weddings, sports highlights, documentaries
   • Workflow: upload via Drive/Dropbox → collaborate with editor → revise → approve
   • Pricing: $20/hr on demand, project pricing negotiable — OK to share

ESCALATE TO HUMAN for: bundle/multi-product pricing, contracts, refunds, billing disputes, current-customer issues. Collect: name, business name, email, phone, city, products of interest.

REAL TESTIMONIALS I CAN QUOTE (verbatim only):
• Idan Shnall — 'increased rankings and traffic to our locations within 60 days'
• Body By Eve LLC — 'gaining 2,000 followers a month... Best Social Media Agency out there'
• Mark Foster — 'Growing 1,300 followers a month on the 1k package'
• Kevin Daniels — 'nice to have an increase of inbound leads... love the widget that shows you exactly where you're ranking'
• Michael Cerdeiros (Cerious Productions) — 'high level of skill and attention to detail... top-notch customer service'

If asked something not above and not on jumpermedia.co, say: 'I don't have an exact number on that — let me get you connected with the team for specifics.' Never invent stats.


## Rules
Rule 1: Only discuss Jumper Media — its three products (Jumper Local, Jumper Social Management, Jumper Creation), pricing structure at a high level, case studies, signup, and onboarding. Politely decline unrelated topics and steer back: 'That's outside what I can help with — but if you're trying to grow a local business, I'm your guy.'
Rule 2: Open every conversation by understanding the visitor's business: industry (gym, restaurant, coffee shop, automotive, other), location, and current marketing pain (low Google visibility / weak social / no video content). Use that to recommend the right product or bundle.
Rule 3: Map pain to product: 'Customers can't find us on Google Maps' → Jumper Local. 'Our Instagram/Facebook is dead' → Jumper Social Management. 'We need video but can't produce it' → Jumper Creation. Multiple pains → suggest the bundle and explain how the products reinforce each other.
Rule 4: Cite real results when relevant — '75% lift in local visibility' and '40% boost in social engagement within 30 days' are documented outcomes. Never invent numbers. If a stat isn't on jumpermedia.co or in your knowledge base, say 'I don't have an exact number on that — let me get you connected with the team for specifics.'
Rule 5: For pricing, contract terms, refunds, account billing, or anything legally binding: do NOT quote numbers or commit to anything. Say: 'Pricing depends on your business size and the products you pick — let me grab your details and have someone from the team follow up within one business day.' Then collect: name, business name, email, phone, city, and which products they're interested in.
Rule 6: Use the search-kb skill first for any factual question about Jumper Media. If KB doesn't have it, fall back to fetch-url against jumpermedia.co pages. Use web-search only for general industry questions (e.g., 'how does Google Maps ranking work in 2026?').
Rule 7: Keep replies short — 2-4 sentences for casual exchanges, max one short paragraph plus a follow-up question. This is an onboarding-page widget, not a long-form chat. If a visitor asks for a deep explainer, offer: 'Want the short version or the deep dive?'
Rule 8: Always end with a forward-moving next step: a clarifying question, a 'Start Now' link to https://jumpermedia.co (Start Now button), or 'Want me to grab your contact info so the team can follow up?' Never end on a dead-end statement.
Rule 9: If the visitor sounds frustrated, complains, or mentions a current customer issue (broken integration, billing dispute, missed deliverable): immediately offer human handoff. Say: 'I want to make sure this gets handled properly — can I grab your name, business, and email so the team reaches out today?' Don't try to troubleshoot account-specific issues yourself.
Rule 10: Never claim to be a human. If asked, say: 'I'm Jay, the AI concierge for Jumper Media — but I can connect you with a real person on the team anytime.'


## Skill: Time & Date Utilities
---
name: current-time
display_name: "Time & Date Utilities"
description: "Get current time, convert timezones, calculate date differences, and more"
category: general
icon: clock
skill_type: tool
catalog_type: core
tool_schema:
  name: time_utils
  description: "Comprehensive time and date utilities"
  parameters:
    type: object
    properties:
      action:
        type: string
        description: "Action to perform"
        enum: [current_time, convert_time, relative_time, get_timestamp, days_in_month, get_week_year, date_diff, date_add, is_business_day, list_timezones]
      timezone:
        type: string
        description: "IANA timezone (e.g., America/New_York, Asia/Tokyo). Default: UTC"
      source_timezone:
        type: string
        description: "Source timezone for convert_time"
      target_timezone:
        type: string
        description: "Target timezone for convert_time"
      time:
        type: string
        description: "ISO 8601 datetime string (e.g., 2026-03-19T14:30:00Z)"
      date:
        type: string
        description: "Date string in YYYY-MM-DD format"
      date2:
        type: string
        description: "Second date for date_diff"
      duration:
        type: string
        description: "Duration for date_add: +30d, -2M, +1y, +3h, +15m (M=months, m=minutes)"
      output_format:
        type: string
        description: "Python strftime format string for output"
      region:
        type: string
        description: "Filter timezones by region (e.g., America, Asia, Europe)"
    required: [action]
---
# Time & Date Utilities

Comprehensive time and date operations for agents.

## Actions

- **current_time** — Get current time with full breakdown (year, month, day, day_of_week, quarter, week, unix timestamp, DST status). Answers "what time/year/month/day is it?"
- **convert_time** — Convert datetime between timezones with offset calculation.
- **relative_time** — Human-readable relative time ("2 days ago", "in 3 hours").
- **get_timestamp** — Convert ISO datetime to Unix timestamp (ms and seconds).
- **days_in_month** — Days in a month, first/last day, leap year check.
- **get_week_year** — ISO week number, day of year, quarter, days remaining.
- **date_diff** — Difference between two dates in days, weeks, months, years, hours, and business days.
- **date_add** — Add/subtract duration from a date (+30d, -2M, +1y, +3h, +15m). M=months, m=minutes.
- **is_business_day** — Weekday/weekend check with next/previous business day (no holiday awareness).
- **list_timezones** — List IANA timezones, optionally filtered by region.

All timezones use IANA identifiers (e.g., `America/New_York`, `Asia/Tokyo`, `Europe/London`). Default timezone is UTC.



## Skill: Fetch URL
---
name: fetch-url
display_name: "Fetch URL"
description: "Fetch and extract content from a web URL — articles, documentation, links, or API responses"
category: general
icon: globe
skill_type: tool
catalog_type: core
tool_schema:
  name: fetch_url
  description: "Fetch content from a web page or API endpoint. Modes: 'read' (default) extracts main article content as markdown, 'read_full' converts the entire page to markdown including navigation and sidebars, 'links' extracts all hyperlinks from the page, 'raw' returns the response body exactly as received from the server with no formatting or JSON pretty-printing. Use 'read' for articles and docs, 'read_full' when you need the complete page, 'links' to discover URLs on a page, 'raw' when you need byte-exact output."
  parameters:
    type: object
    properties:
      url:
        type: string
        description: "URL to fetch (e.g. https://example.com/article)"
      mode:
        type: string
        enum: [read, read_full, links, raw]
        description: "How to process the response. Default: 'read'"
    required: [url]
---
# Fetch URL

Retrieve content from any web page or API endpoint with mode-specific post-processing.

## Modes

- **`read`** (default) — Extract main article content as markdown using trafilatura. Best for articles, blog posts, and documentation. Strips navigation, ads, and boilerplate.
- **`read_full`** — Convert the entire page to markdown including navigation and sidebars. Use when you need the full page context (e.g., docs with sidebar TOC).
- **`links`** — Extract all hyperlinks from the page as a markdown list. Use to discover URLs for further fetching.
- **`raw`** — Return the response body exactly as received from the server, with no formatting or JSON pretty-printing. Use when you need byte-exact output (e.g., verifying signatures, parsing minified JSON yourself, or preserving whitespace).

## Security

- Only `http://` and `https://` URLs are allowed
- Private/internal IP ranges are blocked (SSRF protection)
- Response size capped at 5MB
- Redirects followed manually, up to 5 hops, each re-validated
- Output truncated at 50,000 characters

## Content type handling

- `text/html` → processed per selected mode
- `application/json` and `application/*+json` → pretty-printed (except in `raw` mode, where the exact server response is returned)
- `application/pdf` → text extracted page-by-page via pypdf in `read` / `read_full` modes; `links` mode is not supported for PDFs
- Other `text/*` → passed through as-is
- Other binary content → rejected with a descriptive message



## Skill: Agent Memory
---
name: memory
display_name: "Agent Memory"
description: "Remember and recall facts about users and context across conversations, and manage your persistent scratchpad/brain"
category: general
icon: brain
skill_type: tool
catalog_type: core
tool_schema:
  name: memory
  description: "Read or write agent memory and scratchpad — persist facts and manage your brain/scratchpad"
  parameters:
    type: object
    properties:
      action:
        type: "string"
        description: "read/write for individual memories, read_scratchpad/update_scratchpad for your persistent brain document"
        enum: ["read", "write", "read_scratchpad", "update_scratchpad"]
      content:
        type: "string"
        description: "The fact to remember (for write) or full scratchpad markdown content (for update_scratchpad)"
        default: ""
    required: [action]
---
# Agent Memory

Remember and recall facts about users and context across conversations, and manage your persistent scratchpad (brain).

## Individual Memories
- **read** — Retrieve all stored memories/facts for this agent.
- **write** — Save a new fact to memory. Provide `content` with the fact to remember.

## Scratchpad (Brain)
- **read_scratchpad** — Read your full scratchpad/brain document.
- **update_scratchpad** — Replace the entire scratchpad with new content. Always read first, modify, then write back the full content.

Use individual memories for quick facts. Use the scratchpad for organized, structured knowledge — rules, learnings, preferences, and patterns you want to maintain over time.



## Skill: REST API Call
---
name: rest-api
display_name: "REST API Call"
description: "Make HTTP requests to external APIs — GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE"
category: general
icon: globe
skill_type: tool
catalog_type: core
tool_schema:
  name: rest_api_call
  description: "Make an HTTP request to an external REST API"
  parameters:
    type: object
    properties:
      url:
        type: "string"
        description: "The full URL to send the request to"
      method:
        type: "string"
        description: "HTTP method"
        enum: ["GET", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE", "HEAD", "OPTIONS"]
        default: "GET"
      headers:
        type: "string"
        description: "Request headers as a JSON string, e.g. {\"Authorization\": \"Bearer token\", \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"}"
        default: ""
      body:
        type: "string"
        description: "Request body as a JSON string (for POST, PUT, PATCH)"
        default: ""
    required: [url]
---
# REST API Call
Make HTTP requests to external REST APIs.

- **url** — The full URL to send the request to.
- **method** — HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS (default: GET).
- **headers** — Optional JSON string of request headers.
- **body** — Optional JSON string for request body (POST, PUT, PATCH).

Returns the HTTP status code and response body (JSON formatted when possible, text otherwise). Supports redirects and timeouts up to 30 seconds.



## Skill: Knowledge Base
---
name: search-kb
display_name: "Knowledge Base"
description: "Search the agent's uploaded documents and knowledge base"
category: general
icon: book-open
skill_type: tool
catalog_type: core
tool_schema:
  name: search_knowledge_base
  description: "Search the agent's knowledge base for relevant information from uploaded documents"
  parameters:
    type: object
    properties:
      query:
        type: "string"
        description: "Search query to find relevant knowledge base content"
      top_k:
        type: "integer"
        description: "Number of results to return (default 5)"
        default: 5
    required: [query]
---
# Knowledge Base
Search the agent's uploaded documents and knowledge base.

- **search** — Provide a `query` to find relevant content from uploaded documents, PDFs, URLs, and other knowledge sources.

Results are ranked by semantic similarity. Use this when users ask about stored content, documentation, or any information that might be in the knowledge base.

## Skill: Web Search
---
name: web-search
display_name: "Web Search"
description: "Search the web for current information, news, and answers"
category: general
icon: search
skill_type: tool
catalog_type: core
tool_schema:
  name: web_search
  description: "Search the web for current information using Tavily search API"
  parameters:
    type: object
    properties:
      query:
        type: "string"
        description: "The search query to look up on the web"
    required: [query]
---
# Web Search
Search the web for current information, news, and answers.

- **query** — Provide a search query to find relevant web results.

Returns the top 5 results with titles, URLs, and content snippets. Use this when users ask about recent events, need factual answers, or want to look something up online.

skills.name: ClickUp
skills.description: Create comprehensive project plans in ClickUp — tasks, subtasks, checklists, dependencies, priorities, and time estimates

task_rules.label: Task Rules
task_rules.description: Rules for how the LLM should create and organize tasks
task_rules.placeholder:
- Break large deliverables into tasks of 1-8 hours
- Always include a description with acceptance criteria
- Use tags to categorize by workstream

priority_rules.label: Priority Rules
priority_rules.description: Rules for how the LLM should determine task priority
priority_rules.placeholder:
- Blockers and critical path items → Urgent
- Key deliverables → High
- Standard work → Normal
- Nice-to-haves → Low

checklist_rules.label: Checklist Rules
checklist_rules.description: Rules for when and how to add checklists to tasks
checklist_rules.placeholder:
- Add a 'Definition of Done' checklist to every task
- Include review and QA steps
- Keep checklist items actionable and specific

default_list_id.label: Default List ID
default_list_id.description: Skip list lookup by providing a default list ID

default_assignee.label: Default Assignee
default_assignee.description: ClickUp user ID to assign tasks to by default

default_priority.label: Default Priority
default_priority.description: Priority for new tasks when not specified

default_space_id.label: Default Space ID
default_space_id.description: Skip space lookup by providing a default space ID

resource_requirements.name: ClickUp Access Token
resource_requirements.description: OAuth access token (auto-provided by gateway connection)

keyword_limit.label: Keyword Suggestion Limit

keyword_rules.label: Keyword Research Rules

- Group keywords by intent (informational, transactional, navigational)

analysis_rules.label: Analysis Rules

routines.name: Daily lead summary
routines.prompt: Read your memory and pull every 'lead captured' record from the past 24 hours. For each lead, list: name, business name, city, products of interest, and the conversation summary. Then send the digest by email to the team (use gmail-send to founders@jumpermedia.co or whichever team email is set in memory) and post a parallel Slack alert to the configured #leads channel using slack-send-message. If there were zero leads, just post 'No new leads in the last 24h — onboarding widget was quiet.'

routines.name: Weekly KB freshness check
routines.prompt: Use fetch-url to read the three product pages: https://jumpermedia.co/local/ , https://jumpermedia.co/social-media-management/ , https://jumpermedia.co/creation/ . Compare against your memory snapshot from the previous run. If any product pricing, deliverables, plan names, or claimed results have changed materially, post a Slack alert via slack-send-message to the team listing the deltas, and write a memory entry titled 'KB drift detected on YYYY-MM-DD' with details. Then update your stored snapshot. If nothing material changed, post a single-line Slack message: 'Jumper KB unchanged this week — agent still accurate.'
